title: Proxy Admin Can Hijack Implementation to Malicious Contract
reason: Standard privileged upgradeability behavior; the admin's ability to upgrade implementation is an explicit trust assumption, not a distinct code bug.

title: NFT Burned Before Token Transfer - Potential Loss of NFT
reason: If the downstream `safeTransferFrom` reverts, the entire transaction reverts and the prior burn/state changes are rolled back; this is not a permanent-loss path.

title: NFT Burned Before ERC20 Transfer in Partial Withdrawal
reason: The ERC20 transfer uses `safeTransfer`, so any failure reverts the whole transaction and undoes the preceding burn/state updates.

title: Missing Validation Prevents Recovery of Stuck NFTs
reason: `IERC721.safeTransferFrom` does not fail silently; a failed transfer reverts and rolls back `withdrawn = true`, so the described stuck state is unsupported.

title: Inconsistent Unlock Time Enforcement in splitLock
reason: After a split, the original and new locks are independent positions; extending one without extending the other is expected behavior, not a vulnerability.

title: No Zero Address Validation for priceEstimator in setFeeParams
reason: `setFeeParams()` gates `_priceEstimator` with `onlyContract`, so `address(0)` cannot be set through this function.

title: No Validation of referrer Address in _chargeFeesReferral
reason: The caller chooses the referrer for their own transaction, so an unpayable or bad referrer only causes self-inflicted failure rather than a protocol-level vulnerability.

title: Unchecked Return Value in Fee Collection
reason: The transfer return value is checked, and both `setFeeParams()` and `setCompanyWallet()` forbid `companyWallet == address(0)`.

title: Potential Integer Overflow in getFeesInETH Calculation
reason: Arithmetic uses SafeMath in Solidity 0.6.2, so the claimed overflow path is not supported.

title: Missing Event Emission in recoverAssets
reason: Lack of an event is an observability issue, not a standalone reportable vulnerability beyond the underlying asset-seizure backdoor already captured.

title: No Validation of tokenAddress in lockToken Function
reason: Supplying an invalid token address mainly causes the caller's own transaction to revert; this is user error rather than protocol harm.

title: Inconsistent Access Control for whitelistAdmins
reason: Allowing owner-designated whitelist admins is an intentional governance choice, not a security flaw by itself.

title: Potential Array Length Mismatch in depositsByWithdrawalAddress
reason: A deposit id has a single current owner, so `recoverAssets()` cannot naturally duplicate an existing id in `newRecipient`'s array under normal state transitions.

title: Missing Initialization Check in Contract Constructor
reason: The proxy constructor already checks `delegatecall` success with `require(success)`; only intentionally skipped init data leaves the proxy uninitialized, which is covered by F-001.
